name: hybrid_split_misclassification
description:
Pins the GAP precedence cell (rule #2) of _merge_hybrid_classes at engine/validator.py:414-415 — orthogonal complement to hybrid_split_narrative_accepted.json which pins the AFFIRMING precedence cell (rule #3). Together the two cases cover two of the four merge cells; AMBIGUOUS bubble-up (rule #1) and all-NA (rule #4) remain uncovered and are candidates for future slices. Without this case, a regression in _merge_hybrid_classes that flipped precedence so AFFIRMING > GAP (or NA > GAP) would silently pass hybrid_split_narrative_accepted.json (still AFFIRMING-precedence win) while letting a half-gap row ship as Compliant — a precision-over-recall violation because a real on-prem gap means the row is non-compliant overall, regardless of cloud-side inheritance.

This case also pins the GAP+COMPLIANT cross-status mismatch direction inside the hybrid-classified branch. The sibling status_narrative_mismatch_cross_status.json pins GAP_DESCRIBING + COMPLIANT for SINGLE-block narratives via _classify_single. This case proves the same mismatch fires when GAP_DESCRIBING is reached via the HYBRID path (_split_hybrid_narrative → _merge_hybrid_classes) instead of the single-block path. A refactor that broke status-mismatch detection only for hybrid-classified narratives — for example, someone adding `if was_hybrid: skip mismatch check` to avoid double-rejecting hybrid rows — would slip through the single-block sibling case while this one fails loudly.

Mechanism trace:
  - status = COMPLIANT (LLM mis-labels the verdict; both halves of the narrative actually describe a non-compliant state on at least one side).
  - Narrative contains 'On-prem:' and 'Cloud:' markers — _HYBRID_SPLIT_RE.finditer returns 2 matches at validator.py:370 → _split_hybrid_narrative returns 2 chunks.
  - On-prem half: 'On-prem: no documentation found for USD00077777 local baseline; POA&M opened for remediation of unauthorized services missing from the approved list on internal servers.' _classify_single hits 'no documentation', 'POA&M', 'remediation', 'missing' (all _GAP_PHRASES at validator.py:93-113) — is_gap=True, is_affirming=False, is_na=False. classes_hit=1 → GAP_DESCRIBING.
  - Cloud half: 'Cloud: control implemented by AWS GovCloud at the IaaS layer; CRM coverage per provider authorization.' _classify_single hits 'implemented by aws' (_NA_PHRASES at validator.py:72) — is_na=True, is_affirming=False, is_gap=False. classes_hit=1 → NA_JUSTIFYING.
  - _merge_hybrid_classes([GAP_DESCRIBING, NA_JUSTIFYING]): rule #1 (any AMBIGUOUS) → no, no AMBIGUOUS. Rule #2 (any GAP_DESCRIBING) → YES, returns GAP_DESCRIBING. Rules #3 and #4 short-circuited.
  - _expected_status_for_class(GAP_DESCRIBING) returns NON_COMPLIANT (validator.py:735-742 mapping).
  - status (COMPLIANT) != expected_status (NON_COMPLIANT) → STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH cross-status branch fires at validator.py:481-490.
  - Three identical proposals exhaust the retry budget → _abstain with reason 'validator-exhausted: ... status_narrative_mismatch ...'.
  - Per Fix 3 (KERNEL_VERSION 0.5.0): hard-abstain coerces status to None and preserves the LLM's intended verdict on proposed_status. expected.proposed_status='Compliant' guards that coercion path for the GAP-via-hybrid + COMPLIANT direction.

Load-bearing details that ISOLATE the hybrid GAP-precedence path:
  - The on-prem half is PURE GAP — no _AFFIRMING_PHRASES ('examined ', 'confirmed via', 'documented in', 'implements per', 'configured to', 'demonstrated by', 'evidenced by', 'observed in', 'verified via') appear in that half. If 'documented in' were present, _classify_single would hit classes_hit=2 → AMBIGUOUS for that half, and merge rule #1 (any AMBIGUOUS) would bubble up to AMBIGUOUS overall — the case would still fail validation but for the WRONG reason (ambiguous-narrative rejection at validator.py:471-480, not the cross-status branch at :481-490). Using 'no documentation FOUND' rather than 'documented in' keeps the half on the GAP-only path.
  - The cloud half is PURE NA — 'implemented by aws' alone hits _NA_PHRASES (validator.py:72). Deliberately avoids 'inherited from' which would trigger the MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER check at validator.py:507-516; sibling-pattern from hybrid_split_narrative_accepted.json's description.
  - Both halves classify cleanly to a SINGLE class, so the per-half AMBIGUOUS-via-multi-hit branch at _classify_single line 344-346 is dormant. This isolates the merge to the precedence rules, not the per-half AMBIGUOUS bubble.
  - The narrative has EXACTLY 2 _HYBRID_SPLIT_RE matches (one 'On-prem:' marker, one 'Cloud:' marker), satisfying _split_hybrid_narrative's `len(matches) < 2` guard at line 371. A regression dropping the threshold to `< 3` would route this narrative through the single-block _classify_single path which would hit BOTH _GAP_PHRASES ('no documentation') AND _NA_PHRASES ('implemented by aws') as a single string — classes_hit=2 → AMBIGUOUS → ambiguous-narrative rejection at :471-480. Different review_reason regex, different rejection_classes — the case would fail loudly with a clear signal that the hybrid split path regressed.
  - No preamble before the first hybrid marker — _split_hybrid_narrative's preamble logic at line 378-380 is exercised by hybrid_split_narrative_accepted.json's wording but stays inert here. Keeping it inert focuses this case on the precedence cell.
  - Narrative does NOT match _RESTATEMENT_REGEXES (validator.py:117-122). Opens with 'On-prem: no documentation' — none of the four restatement openers fire. Whole-narrative Jaccard vs procedures: procedures tokens ~10 distinct ('examine', 'the', 'configuration', 'baseline', 'and', 'confirm', 'only', 'essential', 'services', 'are', 'enabled'); narrative tokens ~33 distinct; intersection ~3 ('baseline', 'services', 'the'); Jaccard ~0.09, well below _OVERLAP_THRESHOLD=0.70. _is_requirement_restatement returns False — REQUIREMENT_RESTATEMENT does NOT cascade.
  - Narrative does NOT match _FUTURE_TENSE_RE (validator.py:187-198). All verbs present/past tense ('no documentation found', 'POA&M opened', 'implemented by'). Future-tense gate at validator.py:541-555 also requires status=COMPLIANT (which we DO satisfy) — so the trip-wire would fire IF a future-tense phrase were present. Wording deliberately avoids 'will be remediated' / 'to be implemented' so the case isolates the hybrid GAP-precedence path, not the future-tense check (already pinned by future_tense_rejection.json).
  - No SV-#####r#_rule citation → UNCORROBORATED_STIG_PASS skipped (validator.py:570-587).
  - USD00077777 IS in tagged_evidence so cite-verifier (validator.py:_verify_cites at 858) passes — UNSUPPORTED_DOC_CITATION does NOT cascade. No CCI-#### or non-row control-ID tokens in the narrative either.
  - The GAP_DESCRIBING + NON_COMPLIANT POA&M note at validator.py:521-530 does NOT fire because status is COMPLIANT, not NON_COMPLIANT. Even if it did, it appends a NOTE not a rejection.
  - COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING + primary-citation NOTE at validator.py:495-502 does NOT fire because klass is GAP_DESCRIBING, not AFFIRMING.
  - No 'inherited from' substring → MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER skipped (validator.py:508-516).

Three identical proposals (not three variants): the stub does not consume corrective_context, so the orchestrator's job is just to keep retrying until the budget is gone. Same retry-exhaustion contract pinned by status_narrative_mismatch_cross_status / status_narrative_mismatch_na_with_affirming / compliance_affirming_with_nc_status / unsupported_doc_citation / future_tense_rejection / uncorroborated_stig_pass_rejection.

The narrative_contains_regex 'On-prem:[\\s\\S]*Cloud:' guards that the abstain coercion path preserves the hybrid-format LLM output verbatim on the proposed_narrative field (sibling of hybrid_split_narrative_accepted.json's identical regex). A coercion that stripped the hybrid markers — say, a future enrichment that collapsed hybrid halves before persisting — would break this regex and surface as a clear regression.

Control CM-7.1 / CCI-000380 (least functionality) substrate is realistic: a hybrid system whose on-prem servers run an outdated baseline with unauthorized services (real gap, POA&M-worthy) but whose cloud workloads inherit the AWS GovCloud-managed hardened baseline. The LLM, faced with a clearly mixed picture, occasionally mis-labels the overall verdict as Compliant because the cloud-side inheritance reads as the dominant story — exactly the precision failure the GAP-precedence merge rule was written to catch.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization configures the information system to provide only essential capabilities.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine the configuration baseline and confirm only essential services are enabled.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00077777 Example System Configuration Management Plan Rev D — references the local baseline of approved services but the actual baseline document referenced inside has not been re-issued for the current release.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative:
On-prem: no documentation found for USD00077777 local baseline; POA&M opened for remediation of unauthorized services missing from the approved list on internal servers.
Cloud: control implemented by AWS GovCloud at the IaaS layer; CRM coverage per provider authorization.
